_doc: Tell taxonomy with TWO-ALTITUDE budgets. perPage = how many clear instances are natural on a ~400w page before overuse; book = natural budget across all 202 pages. Thresholds, not elimination: flag overuse and clear off-voice cases, never every instance. Budgets are tunable as rules.md learns.

tells.label: Rule-of-three
tells.desc: Tidy three-part list/cadence ('the cold, the dark, and the silence'). Author's parallelism is uneven/additive — a SMOOTH balanced triad is the tell.

tells.label: Concessive reframe
tells.desc: Negative softened/explained by a positive ('it was hard, but worth it'). Natural in reflection; flag the formulaic 'hard but worth it' shape.

tells.label: Not just X but Y
tells.desc: 'Not only X but Y' escalation scaffold. Flag the scaffold, not every contrast.

tells.label: Participle ending
tells.desc: Sentence ending on a tidy participle phrase ('…, his heart pounding'). Author DOES end on physical buttons — flag the soft emotive participle, not the concrete action beat.

tells.label: Emotion-named
tells.desc: Naming the feeling as a bare ABSTRACT LABEL instead of routing it through body/object/sensory/withholding ('a wave of grief', 'filled with pride', 'exhausted wonder', 'their faces showed despair'). OFF-VOICE. BUT emotion EVOKED physically — even when it names a state ('He was in disbelief.', 'the quiet had a weight to it') — is the author's greatest strength: KEEP. The author's most-rejected tell — bias toward keep, and when flagged the fix must re-route feeling through the body, never delete it.

tells.label: Under-felt moment (ADD emotion)
tells.desc: ADDITIVE, not a removal. A genuine emotional beat the prose skims past flatly or clinically, where an in-voice PHYSICAL or SENSORY beat would let the reader feel they are there. span = the flat line as written; fix = that line reworked or extended with a body/object/sensory/withholding beat in his 19thC register — NEVER an abstract emotion-label, never contradicting the scene's facts. This is the one tell where fix is LONGER than span. RARE and high-confidence only: the moment must clearly deserve to land and currently doesn't. Set additive:true on the flag. The author adds the feeling his way — propose, don't impose.
tells.severityHint: additive — propose sparingly, only on real emotional moments

tells.label: Signposting
tells.desc: Restating a beat the scene already showed, or connective scaffolding ('Moreover', 'and in that moment he realized'). OFF-VOICE — author uses asterisk breaks + hard slugs, not connectors.

tells.label: Period-flat diction
tells.desc: Modern-neutral or therapy/abstract diction where the 19thC setting wants texture ('boundaries', 'processing', 'connection'). HIGH VALUE — loudest AI tell in this book.

tells.label: Repeated opener
tells.desc: 3+ consecutive sentences starting with the same word. Author's clipped 'He swept. He carried.' parallel is intentional — flag only mechanical runs that aren't doing rhythmic work.

tells.label: Adverb speech-tag
tells.desc: 'he said thoughtfully', 'she replied softly', a tag on every line. OFF-VOICE — author uses bare quotes or action beats.

tells.label: "the way a…" simile
tells.desc: Explanatory comparison that tells the reader how to read a gesture by likening it to a generic archetype — 'held them the way a craftsman held his tools', 'looked at it the way he looked at a plat map', 'the way a man does when he…', 'like a [role] would'. A FEW originals are genuinely his (VOICE.md cites craft-metaphor as a signature move), but the construction is now LITTERED across the whole book as AI scaffolding and the author calls it a pure tell. FLAG aggressively and cut HARD: delete the simile and keep the concrete gesture, or replace with one direct beat. Default to flagging — only the rare, truly load-bearing original survives. The `fix` removes the 'the way a X…' clause, not the underlying action.
tells.severityHint: high — overused AI scaffolding, cut by default

tells.label: Summary/lesson line
tells.desc: A thematic takeaway sentence closing a scene ('and that was when everything changed'). OFF-VOICE — author exits on a small physical action, never a moral.
